chapter_context.summary: Pronounces the burden of the Valley of Vision (Jerusalem), rebuking their inappropriate feasting during siege, and prophesying the demotion of Shebna and rise of Eliakim.

outline.text: Inappropriate Revelry of Jerusalem Under Impending Siege
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Inappropriate Revelry of Jerusalem Under Impending Siege (22:1-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Condemnation and Demotion of Proud Treasurer Shebna
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Condemnation and Demotion of Proud Treasurer Shebna (22:15-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Promotion and Future Insecurity of Eliakim
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Promotion and Future Insecurity of Eliakim (22:20-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 22, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
